body.title: Store entries carry machine-checkable validity_conditions; day-close sweep flags stale knowledge — the rot-RATE fix that replaces rebuild-from-scratch
body.text:
# D-memory-validity-conditions-2026-07-07
**title:** Store entries carry machine-checkable validity_conditions; day-close sweep flags stale knowledge
**date:** 2026-07-07
**status:** open
**spawned_tickets:** T-memory-validity-conditions-design, T-validity-sweep-day-close, T-consequence-D-memory-validity-conditions

## Decision narrative
Every documented stale-memory failure (the IMAP claim that repeatedly misled CC, the stale /sorted write-path, lab/ surviving as a write target) shares one root cause: the entry carried no validity conditions — nothing said 'true while the transport is IMAP', so nothing could flag it false when the transport changed. Old memories aren't the problem; memories without validity conditions are. Fix: a validity_conditions field (named, machine-checkable dependencies — an artifact, a path, a fact) on ALL store entry types and auto-memory files, plus a day-close sweep that flags entries whose named dependency changed. Flag = annotation, never deletion (CP2 — stale entries are data). Extends D-decisions-as-guards-anchors-2026-06-25 from decisions to everything and adds detection. Implemented as a field on existing envelopes, not a new artifact type (rules/memory).

**Alternatives rejected:** rebuild-from-scratch with no old memories — resets the rot clock without changing the rot rate, and discards the proof corpus and falsified-hypothesis record, which are the earned assets a fresh Claude cannot re-derive. Rot level is recoverable; rot rate is the design flaw.

## Hypothesis
New/updated store entries carry a validity_conditions field; when a named dependency changes, the day-close sweep flags dependent entries within one day instead of weeks of drift.

## Measurement Signal
Day-close log line: validity sweep: flagged=<N> checked=<M> (printed even when N=0); IMAP-class 'CC misled by stale memory' incidents in session records drop to zero.

## Intention
I intend that stored knowledge carries its validity conditions, so stale memories are flagged by a sweep instead of silently misleading sessions.

## Outcome check
/outcome at first dependency-change event after the sweep ships, or the 2026-07-21 consequence gate, whichever first.

## Design artifact
architecture/cc.0.validity-conditions.20260707.191117757722.json — 7-concept contract (T-memory-validity-conditions-design, closed 2026-07-07). Key choices: 3-type machine-first vocabulary; push (invalidates:) + pull (sweep) detection; annotate-never-delete flags; unresolvable-condition class for meta-rot; v1 probes = grep-only (CP6).
